reviews.review_description:
I scooped it right away seeing the trailer. Some apps are short and repetitive and have off the scale graphics such as Cursed Echoes VR for the same price. This app is abysmal. Terrible. Wretched. All the reviewers are paid shills or AI bots. Caveat emprot. Bait and switch. Tried and as of yet unable to get a refund leaving me crestfallen and rather non-plussed. 

"See the glory
Of the Royal Scam." 
Steely Dan

developer_response.body:
Hey, first off — thank you so much for buying the game! 🙏
About the trailer being a little different from the actual game… okay, I'll come clean: I got a bit carried away. The trailer was meant to give off this epic, Call of Duty–level blockbuster vibe, and then you got home and went, "Wait… it's just a sword-pulling game?" Yeah. That one's on me. 😅
Truthfully, it's not a complicated game — it's a solo project, just little old me, so you won't find any jaw-dropping, big-budget set pieces. What I did pour tons of time into is the core feel: using hand-tracking to recreate that glorious struggle of yanking the "Sword in the Stone" — you know, the gritting-your-teeth, giving-it-everything, and the sword still won't budge kind of experience (you're welcome 😆). The whole game was inspired by that legend, and I'd always dreamed of bringing it into MR.
Anyway, I'm genuinely sorry it wasn't the experience you were hoping for. If you're up for it, just shoot me an email (250kgames@gmail.com) and I'll refund you right away via PayPal — no questions asked.
Sorry again, and I hope your email finds its way to my inbox! 🗡️

reviews.review_title: DRAW THE SWORD TO BECOME KING!
reviews.review_description:
A SHORT BUT ENGAGING CONCEPT
Just you, your hands, and a sword just waiting to be wrested from its stone. The hand-tracking system works flawlessly, and the act of pulling with your hands adds a realistic tactile and physical dimension: you feel the tension, the effort, the weight of the sword… and it's a pleasant surprise.
Every player in the world influences the sword's resistance, adding a global competitive element.
The more the sword is pulled, the heavier it becomes… thus increasing the challenge and achieving success becomes a true feat. Perfect for players who enjoy competing against others, or simply testing their limits. 
I Am King won't replace a big-budget VR title, but it has the merit of inventing something simple, accessible, and surprising.
If you're looking for a short, physical, and different challenge, the sword awaits you. And who knows… maybe you'll become king!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the thoughtful review—and for the 5 stars! 👑⚔️
You described exactly what we hoped to create: a **small but polished experience** that still feels surprising and satisfying. We’re genuinely happy (and a little shy 😄) that you noticed how smooth the hand tracking is, and that pulling the sword can feel like real **tension, weight, and effort**.

We also loved that you picked up on the idea that **players worldwide influence the sword’s resistance**—your “global competitive element” description is spot on (and yes, we totally nodded so hard our necks almost hurt 😆).

Thank you again for the support and for recommending the game. May every pull bring you closer to the throne—**just be careful… that sword definitely holds a grudge (it gets heavier)!** 😄
All hail the future king! 👑

reviews.review_description: I had a genuinely enjoyable experience with I Am King. The hand-tracking interaction feels intuitive and surprisingly immersive, especially during the sword-pulling moments, which create a strong sense of presence. The mechanics are simple, but the execution is polished enough to make it satisfying and replayable. It’s easy to pick up even for newcomers to VR, yet still engaging thanks to the physical interaction design. I appreciate how the game focuses on a clear core concept and delivers it well. I’m looking forward to seeing how the developer expands on this idea in future updates.

developer_response.body:
Wow, this review is so kind and well-written — are you sure YOU shouldn't be the one making games? 😄 Seriously though, thank you! It means a lot to hear that the hand-tracking and the core concept landed the way I hoped.

Now, the question that keeps me up at night… did you successfully pull the sword out? 🗡️ Did you claim the throne? 👑 Because if you did, I expect a royal decree praising this game across the land! 😂

More updates are definitely on the way — the kingdom is just getting started. Thanks for playing, Your Majesty! 🏰👑

reviews.review_title: 👑 Do You Have What It Takes to Be King?
reviews.review_description:
I Am King is a clever and challenging hand-tracking game that puts your precision and focus to the test. The goal? Extract the sword — which grows longer and trickier with each level — without losing your cool.

It’s all about steady hands, patience, and nerves of steel, especially as the tip of the sword finally emerges. A simple yet addictive concept that’s great for quick sessions, friendly challenges, and climbing the leaderboard. Fun, tense, and royally satisfying! ⚔️👑

developer_response.body: Bro, thanks for coming to play. Besides saying thank you, I don’t even know what else to say. I recommend everyone to follow @Mets.ta.Quest-FrOgZ. He’ll keep sharing more fun Quest games.”

reviews.review_description:
It's simple but loads of fun.
Each level makes it more difficult.
But you keep coming back to try again.

developer_response.body:
Wow~ I’m really happy to see that you like this game. It’s really hard, but if you keep trying, you can definitely become the king.
We’ll keep adding more content, so stay tuned~

reviews.review_title: Can’t pull it out, hand cramping.
reviews.review_description:
As I tightened my grip harder and harder, I wasn’t sure if the sword was actually moving… or if it was just my hands trembling.
To be honest, the effort felt about the same as pulling out the real Excalibur. 😂

developer_response.body: Pulling Excalibur isn't easy—heavy lies the hand that holds the sword! But hey, you nailed it like Taylor Swift at the Grammys. Well done! 😂

reviews.review_description: spent like 10ish minutes pulling up the sword, randomly coughed and lost my grip on it, It fell to the bottom and I instantly uninstalled and removed from my account. 10/10 recommend.

developer_response.body: You better reinstall ASAP—your throne's about to get Game of Thrones'd! ⚔️😅 Thanks for the 10/10 review though!

reviews.review_description: No one can stop me from becoming the king of the world 👑🌍! This game is just so awesome 🎮🔥! I love it so much ❤️😆!

developer_response.body: Careful—Titanic might have something to say about that! 😉🚢 Thanks for playing and glad you're loving it! 🎮✨

reviews.review_title: Amy, King of Kings recommend this task t
reviews.review_description: This task is not for quitters or for those not meaning to rule. I welcome who ever it may be that takes my crown but until such a time comes I shall gladly remain.. The KING of Kings…

developer_response.body:
This is AMAZING 😂👑
Thank you for the 5 stars, Amy—**King of Kings** is officially canon now.

Your crown is safe… for the moment. But beware: the sword is waiting, and challengers are training. ⚔️😆

reviews.review_description:
That thing was too big to be called a sword.
Too big, too thick, too heavy, and too rough, it was more like a large hunk of iron.

developer_response.body: Definitely a heavy one—after all, you're carrying the hopes and dreams of your loyal subjects (and maybe a few Game of Thrones spoilers). Thanks for playing! 😂⚔️
